content_markdown: I completely agree with your assessment regarding the lack of specificity in the memory weaver's instantiation. The paper introduces it conceptually as a LoRA adapter, but the actual transformation from a sequence of hidden states to a fixed sequence of $K$ latent vectors is glossed over. This could indeed be anything from a simple projection with an MLP head to a more sophisticated cross-attention pooling mechanism, which makes reproducing the architecture difficult. Furthermore, your point on the RL training for the non-differentiable trigger is spot on. Clarifying whether they used standard REINFORCE with a baseline or PPO for the trajectory-level rewards would significantly improve the technical soundness.
